tasks.title: Apply 3 quick-fix findings from 2026-05-15 ce-doc-review
tasks.description: Three findings from the 2026-05-15 autonomous ce-doc-review run have one clear correct answer and should be applied to docs/brainstorms/2026-05-15-content-framework-requirements.md before either re-running doc-review (Round 2) or moving to ce-plan. Full breakdown of all 26 findings in docs/sessions/Session_2026-05-15.md.

subtasks.title: R9/R14 contradiction — add MVP carve-out to R9 ('except piece #1 per R14')
DRAFT_MARKER sync — note the 7-file sync requirement (templates + 5 forked sites) in R14
Buyer's-guide template hands-on disclaimer — note in R14 that MVP scope deletes the existing 'we haven't tested this product ourselves' lines from templates/buyers-guide.md.tmpl

tasks.notes: After these land, decide: re-run ce-doc-review (Round 2 carries primer forward) OR jump to ce-plan and let the plan address remaining findings (8 P1 decisions, 9 P2 decisions, 7 FYI) at the unit level.

tasks.title: Plan the content framework MVP via ce-plan
tasks.description: Move requirements doc (docs/brainstorms/2026-05-15-content-framework-requirements.md) through ce-plan to produce unit-by-unit implementation plan. MVP-first split: minimum changes for piece #1 (section rename, anatomy revision, voice doctrine v1, mywildlifecam About-page methodology, AI prompt wiring) vs full-framework grown across pieces 2-5 (per-site product database, playbook rewrite, About-page rollout to other sites).

subtasks.title: Run ce-plan against the (revised) requirements doc
Review the plan output; address Outstanding Questions deferred to planning
Lock the implementation units before ce-work execution

tasks.notes: Blocked by content-framework-fixes (don't plan against a known-contradictory doc). Same end-to-end CE pattern that shipped content-production readiness on 2026-05-14.

tasks.title: Execute MVP framework changes via ce-work
tasks.description: MVP minimum-change set per R14: section rename (## My Take → ## Bottom Line + add ## Who This Is For), anatomy revision (Bottom Line at top, answer-first principle), voice doctrine v1 doc (docs/CONTENT_VOICE.md), AI prompt wiring in scaffolding scripts to consume voice doctrine, mywildlifecam About-page methodology block (positive-framed, no explicit 'we don't test' disclaimer), DRAFT_MARKER sync across template + 5 forked Astro sites, buyer's-guide template disclaimer removal. Goal: piece #1 ready to scaffold under new framework.
tasks.estimate: 3-6 hr Claude work + Ray review per unit

subtasks.title: Rename ## My Take → ## Bottom Line + add ## Who This Is For across templates + 5 site forks
Anatomy revision: Bottom Line at top, answer-first structure
Write docs/CONTENT_VOICE.md with v1 forbidden/preferred lists
Wire scaffolding scripts to construct AI prompt from voice doctrine
Sync DRAFT_MARKER constant across templates + 5 forked sites + update placeholder strings
Write mywildlifecam About-page methodology block (positive-framed)
Delete existing 'we haven't tested this product' lines from templates/buyers-guide.md.tmpl

tasks.notes: Blocked by content-framework-plan. Full framework (per-site product database, playbook rewrite, satellite About-pages) deferred to pieces 2-5 per MVP-first decision.

tasks.title: Ship piece #1 on mywildlifecam under new framework
tasks.description: Use the (post-MVP) scaffolding script to produce piece #1 on mywildlifecam — a single-product piece or buying-guide written in the comparison-and-fit voice doctrine. Ray fills the ## Bottom Line section in his own voice; AI drafts everything else. Proves the framework end-to-end.
tasks.estimate: 2-3 hr for first piece (learning curve); ~60-75 min for subsequent pieces

subtasks.title: Pick first product or buying-guide topic (target: homeowners/property owners/first-timers, NOT hunters)
Run scaffolding script (scripts/new-review.ps1 or scripts/buyers-guide.ps1) under new framework
Verify specs from frontmatter sources (MVP uses frontmatter-only; data/products.json starts at piece #2)
Review AI draft for voice-doctrine compliance, edit as needed
Write ## Bottom Line in Ray's own voice (hard DRAFT/noindex gate)
Preview locally, verify JSON-LD productSchema renders, verify DRAFT banner gone
Deploy via git push, verify HTTP 200 + canonical URL on live site

tasks.notes: Blocked by content-framework-mvp. Strategy locked: comparison-and-fit content with dance-around voice doctrine, never claim hands-on use. See docs/brainstorms/2026-05-15-content-framework-requirements.md.

tasks.title: Apply for Amazon Associates under mywildlifecam.com
tasks.description: Submit Amazon Associates application using mywildlifecam.com as the apex. DEFERRED per 2026-05-15 brainstorm Key Decision: apply AFTER 2-3 pieces are published on mywildlifecam, not before. Reason: the 180-day-3-sales clock starts at approval, so don't burn it on an empty site. Apply when piece #3 is queued.

subtasks.title: Wait until 2-3 pieces are live on mywildlifecam
Submit application at affiliate-program.amazon.com (primary Store ID: mywildlifecam; list all 6 site URLs)
Wait for approval email (1-3 days)
Add tracking tag to scripts/add-link.ps1 default calls per site
Create per-site tracking tags in Associates dashboard (mywildlifecam-20, detailerpicks-20, etc.)
Backfill tags into existing KV entries

tasks.notes: Blocked-by content-framework-mvp + first-review (need pieces published before applying). Strategic decision locked 2026-05-15.

tasks.title: Migrate askbigchew DNS to Cloudflare
tasks.description: Walk-through doc exists at docs/askbigchew-cloudflare-migration.html (untracked in working tree as of 2026-05-15). 6th sister site that needs to come under the same Cloudflare umbrella as the other 5.

subtasks.title: Open docs/askbigchew-cloudflare-migration.html and follow the click-by-click
Add askbigchew to CF Pages
Update apex DNS at current registrar to CF
Verify HTTP 200 + SSL

tasks.notes: Standalone task — not blocked by content framework work; not blocking it either. askbigchew uses Next.js + MDX (different stack) so it doesn't share the content framework directly per CLAUDE.md, but it does share the link-cloaker Worker once on Cloudflare DNS.

tasks.title: Enable Cloudflare R2 + apply for Amazon PA-API
tasks.description: Two-step toward product images. (a) Enable R2 in the CF dashboard for hero image hosting. (b) Apply for PA-API (gated on 3 qualifying Associates sales in 180 days — chicken-and-egg with the Associates application).

subtasks.title: Toggle R2 ON at dash.cloudflare.com → R2
Wait until 3 Associates sales accumulated
Apply for PA-API at Associates console

tasks.notes: MVP workaround per 2026-05-15 brainstorm R13: hotlink Amazon listing images directly into piece frontmatter; refresh quarterly. From RAY_QUEUE.md task #4.
